summary: Maintaining adequate urine output through regular measurement provides a more consistent marker of effective fluid resuscitation compared to alternatives such as central venous pressure due to better correlations with overall circulatory status and easier ongoing assessment during critical care scenarios. Thus, \boxed{\text{Urine Output}} remains the preferred choice based on robust evidence supporting its use across various medical conditions requiring careful hydration management.
